questions.question: Explain the meaning of climate.
questions.answer: Climate refers to the sum total of weather conditions and variations over a large area for a long period of time.

questions.question: Explain the difference between weather and climate.
questions.answer: Weather refers to the condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time, while climate refers to the average weather conditions of a place over a long period.

questions.question: Explain the elements of weather and climate.
questions.answer: The elements of weather and climate include temperature, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity and precipitation.

questions.question: Explain why India has a monsoon type of climate.
questions.answer: India has a monsoon type of climate because seasonal winds known as monsoons bring rainfall to most parts of the country.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of monsoon.
questions.answer: Monsoon refers to the seasonal reversal of wind direction during a year.

questions.question: Explain the role of latitude in determining climate.
questions.answer: Latitude affects the amount of solar energy received at a place and influences its temperature.

questions.question: Explain how altitude influences climate.
questions.answer: As altitude increases, temperature decreases, making mountainous regions cooler.

questions.question: Explain how the Himalayas influence the climate of India.
questions.answer: The Himalayas act as a barrier preventing cold winds from Central Asia and also cause rainfall by blocking monsoon winds.

questions.question: Explain the concept of continentality.
questions.answer: Continentality refers to extreme climate conditions experienced in places far from the sea.

questions.question: Explain the cold weather season in India.
questions.answer: The cold weather season occurs from mid-November to February and is characterized by low temperatures and dry weather.

questions.question: Explain the hot weather season in India.
questions.answer: The hot weather season lasts from March to May and is characterized by high temperatures and hot winds.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of loo winds.
questions.answer: Loo are strong, hot and dry winds that blow over northern India during summer.

questions.question: Explain the advancing monsoon season.
questions.answer: The advancing monsoon season begins in early June when southwest monsoon winds bring rainfall to most parts of India.

questions.question: Explain the retreating monsoon season.
questions.answer: Retreating monsoon occurs during October and November when monsoon winds withdraw from India.

questions.question: Explain why Tamil Nadu receives rainfall in winter.
questions.answer: Tamil Nadu receives rainfall during winter due to retreating monsoon winds and cyclonic storms from the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the distribution of rainfall in India.
questions.answer: Rainfall in India varies greatly from region to region due to monsoon winds and relief features.

questions.question: Explain why northeastern India receives heavy rainfall.
questions.answer: Moist monsoon winds strike the Khasi Hills causing heavy rainfall.

questions.question: Explain why Rajasthan receives low rainfall.
questions.answer: Rajasthan receives low rainfall because it lies far from the sea and monsoon winds lose moisture before reaching it.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of break in monsoon.
questions.answer: Break in monsoon refers to short intervals during the rainy season when rainfall temporarily stops.

questions.question: Explain the causes of floods in India.
questions.answer: Floods occur due to heavy monsoon rainfall and overflowing rivers.

questions.question: Explain the causes of droughts in India.
questions.answer: Droughts occur when regions receive very little rainfall for long periods.

questions.question: Explain the importance of monsoon for agriculture.
questions.answer: Monsoon rainfall provides water necessary for crop cultivation.

questions.question: Explain the importance of monsoon for India's economy.
questions.answer: The monsoon influences agriculture, water resources and economic activities in India.

questions.question: Explain how climate affects the lifestyle of people.
questions.answer: Climate influences people's food, clothing, housing and occupations.

questions.question: Explain why monsoon is called a unifying bond.
questions.answer: Monsoon influences agriculture and life across India, connecting different regions through a common seasonal cycle.
